sourceText: The hardships of his youth fueled his unflinching portrayal of societal ills. He used his writing to expose injustice and found solace and connection through public readings and theatrical performances.
translatedText: Wahalhalun kuruciyarsa ya kara rura wutar siffanta matsalolin da al'umma ke fuskanta. Ya yi amfani da rubutunsa wajen fallasa rashin adalci da samun nutsuwa da alaƙa ta hanyar karatun jama'a da wasan kwaikwayo.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Clara Barton
translatedText: Clara Barton

sourceText: She suffered from poor health from childhood and endured immense physical and emotional suffering during the Civil War. She also grappled with the aftermath of war and personal tragedies.
translatedText: Ta yi fama da rashin lafiya tun lokacin ƙuruciya kuma ta sha wahala mai yawa ta jiki da ta rai a lokacin Yaƙin Basasa. Ta kuma yi fama da abubuwan da suka biyo bayan yaƙe-yaƙe da bala'o'i.

sourceText: She transformed her suffering into a driving force to help others. Drawing on her wartime experiences, she established a systematic nursing system and, through Red Cross activities, practiced humanitarianism worldwide, healing her own wounds.
translatedText: Ta canza wahalarta ta zama abin motsa jiki don taimakon wasu. Yin la'akari da abubuwan da ta samu a lokacin yaƙi, ta kafa tsarin jinya mai tsauri kuma, ta ayyukan Red Cross, ta yi aikin jin kai a duk duniya, ta warkar da raunukanta.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Despite being one of the foremost scholars of his time, Leibniz was often embroiled in academic rivalries and political intrigues. The priority dispute over calculus with Newton, in particular, significantly impacted his reputation. Furthermore, his philosophical concept of the best of all possible worlds was frequently misunderstood or criticized.
translatedText: Duk da kasancewarsa daya daga cikin manyan malamai na zamaninsa, Leibniz ya kasance yana shagaltuwa da fafatawa a fannin ilimi da hada-hadar siyasa. Rikicin fifiko kan lissafin lissafi tare da Newton, musamman, ya yi tasiri sosai ga sunansa. Bugu da ƙari, ra'ayin falsafarsa na mafi kyawun duk duniya mai yuwuwa an yi rashin fahimta akai-akai ko suka.

sourceText: He overcame these challenges through unwavering academic inquiry and extensive scholarly work. His deep analysis of complex problems and insights across numerous disciplines shone brighter with time, profoundly influencing subsequent generations of scholars.
translatedText: Ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar bincike na ilimi da yawa da ayyukan ilimi. Bincikensa mai zurfi game da rikitattun matsaloli da hangen nesa a cikin fannonin ilimi da yawa sun haskaka da lokaci, tare da yin tasiri sosai ga ƙarni na gaba na masana.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: The relentless challenges of seafaring, the constant threat of disease, encounters with indigenous peoples, and his own tragic demise were significant pains. During his second Pacific voyage, he witnessed firsthand the devastating effects of scurvy on his crew.
translatedText: Kalubalen da ake fama da shi na safarar teku, da barazanar kamuwa da cuta, da saduwa da ƴan asalin ƙasar, da nasa mummunar mutuwarsa sun kasance masu radadi. A lokacin tafiyarsa ta biyu ta tekun Pacific, ya gane wa idonsa mummunan illar scurvy a kan ma'aikatan jirginsa.

sourceText: Cook meticulously planned his expeditions, incorporating scientific methodologies and prioritizing crew health. He implemented innovative measures such as ensuring the consumption of fresh produce and maintaining strict hygiene standards to combat diseases like scurvy.
translatedText: Cook ya tsara tafiye-tafiyensa sosai, yana haɗa hanyoyin kimiyya da ba da fifiko ga lafiyar ma'aikatan jirgin. Ya aiwatar da sabbin matakai kamar tabbatar da amfani da sabbin kayan amfanin gona da kiyaye tsauraran matakan tsafta don yaƙar cututtuka kamar scurvy.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Meister Eckhart
translatedText: Sunan mahaifi Eckhart

sourceText: Eckhart's profound theological ideas often clashed with the orthodox doctrines of the Church, placing him at risk of being accused of heresy. His concepts, such as the 'birth of God in the soul' and the 'detachment of the soul,' were frequently misunderstood, leading to scrutiny and condemnation.
translatedText: Zurfafan ra'ayoyin tauhidi na Eckhart sau da yawa suna cin karo da koyaswar Ikilisiya na Orthodox, suna sanya shi cikin haɗarin zarge shi da bidi'a. Ra'ayoyinsa, kamar 'haihuwar Allah a cikin rai' da 'keɓewar rai,' yawanci ana kuskuren fahimtar su, suna haifar da bincike da hukunci.

sourceText: Despite facing accusations and potential persecution, Eckhart remained steadfast in his philosophical and spiritual convictions. He meticulously refined his teachings, employing rich metaphorical language and deep spiritual insight to clarify his message. His persistent pursuit of understanding and clear articulation laid the groundwork for the lasting impact and widespread study of his thought.
translatedText: Duk da fuskantar tuhume-tuhume da tsangwama, Eckhart ya tsaya tsayin daka a cikin hukunce-hukuncen falsafa da na ruhaniya. Ya ƙware koyarwarsa sosai, yana amfani da yare na kwatanci da zurfin fahimta ta ruhaniya don fayyace saƙonsa. Yunƙurinsa na neman fahimta da fayyace fayyace ya sa harsashin tasiri mai ɗorewa da kuma yaɗuwar nazarin tunaninsa.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Vitus Bering
translatedText: Vitus Bering

sourceText: Extreme cold, starvation, disease in unfamiliar lands, the threat of mutiny from his crew, and uncertain support from the Russian crown made Bering's expeditions exceedingly difficult.
translatedText: Matsananciyar sanyi, yunwa, cututtuka a ƙasashen da ba a sani ba, barazanar ɓarkewa daga ma'aikatan jirginsa, da rashin tabbas daga kambin Rasha ya sa balaguron Bering ya kasance mai wahala.

sourceText: Bering overcame these challenges with an iron will, exceptional navigation skills, and the cooperation of his men. His persistent efforts culminated in the monumental achievement of charting Russia's northeastern coastline.
translatedText: Bering ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen da ƙarfin ƙarfe, ƙwarewar kewayawa na musamman, da haɗin gwiwar mutanensa. Yunkurin da ya yi ya kai ga gagarumin nasarar da aka samu na tsara gabar tekun arewa maso gabashin Rasha.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Bartolomeu Dias
translatedText: Bartolomeu Dias

sourceText: The relentless Westerlies, the dread of unknown waters, the wavering morale of my crew, and the constant scarcity of provisions were burdens I always had to bear.
translatedText: Westerlies marasa jajircewa, tsoron ruwan da ba a san su ba, da rugujewar halin ma'aikatan jirgina, da karancin abinci akai-akai sun kasance nauyi na koyaushe.

sourceText: Facing the boundless ocean, I placed my faith in divine providence and human will. My courage to face forward, rather than turn back, ultimately revealed a new path.
translatedText: Ina fuskantar teku mara iyaka, na sanya bangaskiyata cikin tanadin Allah da nufin ɗan adam. Jajircewa na don fuskantar gaba, maimakon komawa baya, a ƙarshe ya bayyana sabuwar hanya.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Edward Jenner
translatedText: Edward Jenner

sourceText: In the late 18th century, smallpox was a terrifying scourge, claiming countless lives globally. With no effective cure, people lived in fear, and the toll on children was particularly devastating. As a physician, witnessing such suffering was a profound anguish.
translatedText: A ƙarshen karni na 18, ƙwayar cuta ta kasance annoba mai ban tsoro, tana yin asarar rayuka marasa adadi a duniya. Ba tare da ingantaccen magani ba, mutane suna rayuwa cikin tsoro, kuma adadin yara ya yi muni sosai. A matsayin likita, shaida irin wannan wahalar baƙin ciki ne ƙwarai.

sourceText: I observed that milkmaids who contracted cowpox seemed immune to smallpox. This led me to hypothesize that exposure to the milder cowpox could confer immunity. In 1796, I tested this by inoculating a young boy, James Phipps, with cowpox matter. When Phipps later showed no ill effects after being exposed to smallpox, my hypothesis was validated. This marked a significant breakthrough in our fight against smallpox.
translatedText: Na lura cewa masu aikin nono da suka kamu da cutar sankara ba su da lafiya. Wannan ya sa na yi hasashe cewa fallasa zuwa ga mafi ƙarancin saniya na iya ba da rigakafi. A shekara ta 1796, na gwada hakan ta hanyar yiwa wani matashi, James Phipps, maganin cutar sankarau. Lokacin da Phipps daga baya bai nuna rashin lafiya ba bayan an fallasa shi ga ƙananan yara, hasashe na ya inganta. Wannan ya nuna gagarumin ci gaba a yaƙin da muke yi da cutar sankarau.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Emily Dickinson
translatedText: Emily Dickinson

sourceText: Dickinson experienced profound loneliness, grief from the loss of loved ones, and the societal constraints placed upon women in her era. She increasingly withdrew from society, particularly from the late 1850s onward, becoming a recluse.
translatedText: Dickinson ya sami kaɗaici mai zurfi, baƙin ciki daga asarar ƙaunatattunsa, da kuma matsalolin al'umma da aka sanya mata a zamaninta. Ta ƙara janyewa daga al'umma, musamman daga ƙarshen 1850s gaba, ta zama mai rahusa.

sourceText: She transformed these internal struggles into her art. Dickinson used poetry as a powerful vehicle to explore her inner life, the natural world, and the profound mysteries of life and death. Her intense focus on writing allowed her to transmute her solitude into creative energy and timeless verse.
translatedText: Ta canza waɗannan gwagwarmaya na cikin gida zuwa fasaharta. Dickinson ta yi amfani da waƙa a matsayin abin hawa mai ƙarfi don bincika rayuwarta ta ciki, duniyar halitta, da kuma manyan asirai na rayuwa da mutuwa. Tsananin mayar da hankalinta ga rubuce-rubuce ya ba ta damar canza zamanta cikin kuzarin kirkira da aya mara lokaci.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: The severe illness that led to his deafness in later life plunged Goya into profound isolation and despair. He also experienced immense psychological suffering witnessing the brutality of war, political upheaval, and the threat of the Inquisition in Spain, observing human cruelty and societal absurdities.
translatedText: Mugunyar rashin lafiya da ta kai shi ga kurma a rayuwarsa ta jefa Goya cikin tsananin keɓewa da yanke kauna. Har ila yau, ya sha wahala mai yawa na tunani yana shaida zalunci na yaki, tashin hankali na siyasa, da kuma barazanar Inquisition a Spain, yana lura da zaluncin ɗan adam da rashin tausayi na al'umma.

sourceText: Despite the physical pain of deafness, Goya delved deeper into his inner world, transforming his experiences into art. Through experimental and intense works like the 'Black Paintings' series, he expressed his suffering and the tragedies of his time, forging an artistic breakthrough. He maintained his resolve as an artist, never abandoning his brush even during his exile.
translatedText: Duk da ciwon jiki na kurma, Goya ya zurfafa cikin duniyar cikinsa, yana mai da abubuwan da ya samu zuwa fasaha. Ta hanyar gwaje-gwaje da ayyuka masu tsanani kamar jerin 'Baƙaƙen zane-zane', ya bayyana wahalarsa da masifun zamaninsa, ya ƙirƙira ci gaban fasaha. Ya ci gaba da ƙudirinsa a matsayinsa na mai fasaha, ba ya barin goga ko da a lokacin da yake gudun hijira.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: George Eliot
translatedText: George Eliot

sourceText: Mary Ann Evans
translatedText: Mary Ann Evans

sourceText: She faced significant social ostracism and criticism in Victorian society for her unconventional relationship with George Henry Lewes, a married man. This, combined with the societal constraints placed upon intellectual women of her time, led to profound personal struggles.
translatedText: Ta fuskanci tsananin kyama da suka a cikin al'ummar Victoria saboda dangantakarta da ba ta dace ba da George Henry Lewes, mutumin aure. Wannan, haɗe da matsalolin al'umma da aka sanya wa mata masu hankali na zamaninta, sun haifar da gwagwarmaya mai zurfi.

sourceText: Eliot channeled her intellect and passion into her writing, producing some of the most insightful novels of the era. Her pseudonym allowed her work to be judged independently of her gender. Her enduring partnership with Lewes provided intellectual companionship and emotional support, enabling her to overcome societal disapproval and focus on her literary achievements.
translatedText: Eliot ta ba da hankali da sha'awarta a cikin rubuce-rubucenta, ta samar da wasu mafi kyawun litattafai na zamanin. Sunanta ya ba da damar a tantance aikinta ba tare da jinsinta ba. Haɗin gwiwarta na jurewa da Lewes ya ba da abokantaka na hankali da goyan bayan rai, yana ba ta damar shawo kan rashin amincewar al'umma da mai da hankali kan nasarorin da ta samu na adabi.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Henry Hudson
translatedText: Henry Hudson

sourceText: Despite his conviction in his expeditions, he faced mutiny from his crew and the daunting fear of the unknown seas. Ultimately, he failed to find the Northwest Passage and met a tragic end.
translatedText: Duk da hukuncin da aka yanke masa a balaguron da ya yi, ya gamu da tsautsayi daga ma'aikatansa da kuma tsananin tsoron tekun da ba a sani ba. A ƙarshe, ya kasa gano hanyar Arewa maso Yamma kuma ya gamu da mummunan ƙarshe.

sourceText: Even amidst setbacks and betrayal, he never lost his spirit of relentless challenge towards the unknown world. Although his journey did not end in personal success, it contributed to redrawing the map of North America.
translatedText: Ko da a cikin koma baya da cin amana, bai taɓa rasa ruhunsa na ƙalubalen ƙalubale ga duniyar da ba a sani ba. Ko da yake tafiyarsa ba ta ƙare cikin nasara na kansa ba, ta ba da gudummawa ga sake zana taswirar Arewacin Amurka.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Much of Homer's life is shrouded in mystery, and his very existence is debated. Legends of blindness suggest a profound personal struggle.
translatedText: Yawancin rayuwar Homer ba ta cikin sirri, kuma ana tafka muhawara game da kasancewarsa. Tatsuniyoyi na makanta suna ba da shawarar gwagwarmayar mutum mai zurfi.

sourceText: Despite potential physical impairment, he possessed extraordinary inner vision, drawing inspiration to craft timeless epics that have resonated through millennia.
translatedText: Duk da yuwuwar tawayar jiki, yana da hangen nesa na ban mamaki na ciki, yana jawo zaburarwa ga ƙera almara maras lokaci waɗanda suka mamaye cikin shekaru aru-aru.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Jane Austen
translatedText: Jane Austen

sourceText: It took a considerable time to gain recognition for her talent as a writer, and she faced many restrictions due to her gender when publishing her works. She also experienced financial difficulties and family health issues.
translatedText: Ya ɗauki lokaci mai yawa don samun karɓuwa ga gwaninta a matsayin marubuci, kuma ta fuskanci hani da yawa saboda jinsinta lokacin buga ayyukanta. Ta kuma fuskanci matsalolin kuɗi da matsalolin lafiyar iyali.

sourceText: She persevered by consistently writing and receiving support from her family and friends. Her unique literary world was built upon keen observation of society and a profound understanding of human nature.
translatedText: Ta dage ta hanyar rubuce-rubuce akai-akai tare da samun tallafi daga danginta da abokanta. Duniyar adabinta na musamman an gina ta ne bisa kyakkyawar lura da al'umma da kuma zurfin fahimtar yanayin ɗan adam.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: John Stuart Mill
translatedText: John Stuart Mill

sourceText: Mill endured a rigorous, intellectually demanding upbringing by his father, James Mill, which led to significant emotional and psychological strain. He also experienced a severe bout of depression in his early twenties.
translatedText: Mill ya jimre da ƙayyadaddun ilimi, neman ilimi daga mahaifinsa, James Mill, wanda ya haifar da tsananin damuwa da tunani. Ya kuma fuskanci matsananciyar damuwa a farkon shekarunsa ashirin.

sourceText: Despite these challenges, he found solace and emotional release in literature and the arts. His profound intellectual and emotional connection with Harriet Taylor was instrumental in helping him rediscover meaning in life and overcome his difficulties.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan ƙalubalen, ya sami kwanciyar hankali da sakin tunani a cikin adabi da fasaha. Zurfafan tunaninsa da haɗin kai da Harriet Taylor ya taimaka masa wajen sake gano ma'anar rayuwa da shawo kan matsalolinsa.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: As a woman, her political voice was suppressed, and her radical ideas led to her trial and execution during the Reign of Terror.
translatedText: A matsayinta na mace, an danne muryarta ta siyasa, kuma ra'ayoyinta masu tsattsauran ra'ayi sun kai ga fitina da kisa a lokacin Mulkin Ta'addanci.

sourceText: Her courageous writings and actions inspired later feminist movements, and her ahead-of-her-time ideas are remembered to this day.
translatedText: Rubuce-rubucenta masu ƙarfin hali da ayyukanta sun ƙarfafa ƙungiyoyin mata daga baya, kuma ana tunawa da tunaninta na gaba-gaba har wa yau.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Living through the turbulent period of the late Roman Empire, he faced academic disputes and misunderstandings from fellow philosophers while structuring his own thought. Furthermore, he encountered difficulties in widely disseminating his philosophical teachings within the empire.
translatedText: Rayuwa a cikin lokacin tashin hankali na marigayi Roman Empire, ya fuskanci takaddama na ilimi da rashin fahimta daga abokan falsafa yayin da yake tsara tunaninsa. Bugu da ƙari, ya ci karo da wahalhalu wajen yaɗa koyarwarsa ta falsafa a cikin daular.

sourceText: Despite these challenges, he remained steadfast, pursuing union with the 'One' through inner reflection and meditation. His student Porphyry compiled his lecture notes into the 'Enneads,' ensuring his philosophy was fully transmitted to posterity.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan ƙalubalen, ya kasance da tsayin daka, yana neman haɗin kai da 'Daya' ta hanyar tunani na ciki da tunani. Dalibinsa Porphyry ya tattara bayanan laccocinsa cikin 'Enneads,' yana tabbatar da cewa falsafarsa ta kasance cikakke ga zuriya.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Roald Amundsen
translatedText: Roald Amundsen ne adam wata

sourceText: Initial expedition hardships, financial struggles, and the harsh, isolating environment of the poles were constant torments. The failed attempt to reach the North Pole and the pressure of the race to the South Pole were significant mental trials.
translatedText: Wahalhalun balaguro na farko, gwagwarmayar kuɗi, da ƙaƙƙarfan yanayi na ware sanduna azaba ce ta yau da kullun. Ƙoƙarin da bai yi nasara ba na isa Pole ta Arewa da kuma matsin lambar tseren zuwa Kudancin Kudu sun kasance manyan gwaji na hankali.

sourceText: Meticulous planning, adoption of innovative technologies (airships, airplanes), and above all, an indomitable will allowed him to overcome difficulties. He viewed failures as learning opportunities rather than setbacks, relentlessly pursuing his goals.
translatedText: Tsare-tsare mai zurfi, ɗaukar sabbin fasahohi (jirgin sama, jiragen sama), kuma sama da duka, wanda ba zai iya jurewa ba zai ba shi damar shawo kan matsaloli. Ya ɗauki gazawa a matsayin damar koyo maimakon koma baya, yana ci gaba da bin manufofinsa.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Elizabeth Cady Stanton
translatedText: Elizabeth Cady Stanton

sourceText: Women were legally considered property of their husbands, denied rights to property, child custody, and even their own earnings. Their voices were socially silenced, and educational opportunities were severely limited.
translatedText: A bisa doka an dauki mata tamkar mallakar mazajensu, an hana su haƙƙin mallakar dukiya, riƙon yara, har ma da abin da suke samu. Muryoyinsu sun yi shiru a cikin jama'a, kuma damar ilimi ta yi iyaka sosai.

sourceText: I was outraged by this injustice and recognized women as intellectual and moral equals to men. Alongside my peers, I drafted the 'Declaration of Sentiments,' ceaselessly speaking and writing to change public perception and demand our inherent rights.
translatedText: Na fusata da wannan rashin adalci na kuma gane mata a matsayin masu hankali da tarbiyya daidai da maza. Tare da takwarorina, na tsara 'Sanarwar Hankali,' ba tare da tsayawa yin magana da rubutu ba don canza ra'ayin jama'a da neman haƙƙoƙinmu na asali.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Henrik Ibsen
translatedText: Henrik Ibsen

sourceText: He suffered immense financial hardship due to his father's business failures in his youth, and later faced debt collection and societal prejudice as a young man. These struggles deeply influenced his work, infusing it with dark and realistic themes.
translatedText: Ya sha fama da matsananciyar wahala sakamakon gazawar kasuwancin mahaifinsa a lokacin kuruciyarsa, daga baya kuma ya fuskanci tarin basussuka da kyamar al'umma tun yana matashi. Waɗannan gwagwarmaya sun yi tasiri sosai a cikin aikinsa, suna shigar da shi da batutuwa masu duhu da gaske.

sourceText: Despite these hardships, Ibsen honed his craft through writing. He keenly observed societal absurdities and exposed them through the medium of theatre, exploring both social and personal truths. His plays gradually gained recognition, bringing him financial stability and renown.
translatedText: Duk da waɗannan wahalhalu, Ibsen ya haɓaka fasaharsa ta hanyar rubutu. Ya lura da rashin hankali na al'umma kuma ya fallasa su ta hanyar wasan kwaikwayo, yana binciken gaskiyar zamantakewa da na sirri. A hankali wasanninsa sun sami karbuwa, wanda hakan ya kawo masa kwanciyar hankali da kuma shahara.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: He suffered from chronic health issues, particularly digestive and respiratory ailments. He also grappled with the potential political exploitation of his literary works during a time of great civil unrest.
translatedText: Ya sha fama da matsalolin lafiya na yau da kullun, musamman cututtukan narkewar abinci da na numfashi. Ya kuma kokarta da yuwuwar amfani da ayyukan adabinsa na siyasa a lokacin babban tashin hankalin al'umma.

sourceText: He found solace and focus in nature and his poetry, channeling his struggles into his art. The support of his friends and patrons, like Maecenas, was crucial in his perseverance to complete his masterpieces.
translatedText: Ya sami kwanciyar hankali da mai da hankali a cikin yanayi da kuma waƙarsa, yana ba da gwagwarmayar gwagwarmayarsa a cikin fasaharsa. Taimakon abokansa da majiɓintansa, kamar Maecenas, yana da mahimmanci a jajircewarsa don kammala manyan ayyukansa.

sourceText: Giants of History
translatedText: Kattai na Tarihi

sourceText: Eleanor of Aquitaine
translatedText: Eleanor na Aquitaine

sourceText: The breakdown of her marriage to Louis VII, the failures of the Second Crusade, conflicts with her second husband Henry II, and her lengthy imprisonment ordered by Henry II due to her support of her sons' rebellions.
translatedText: Rushewar aurenta da Louis VII, gazawar Crusade na biyu, ya ci karo da mijinta na biyu Henry II, da kuma ɗaurin ɗaurin da aka yi mata na tsawon lokaci da Henry II ya umarta saboda ta goyi bayan tawayen 'ya'yanta.

sourceText: Upon her release from imprisonment, she retained significant political influence, acting as regent for her son Richard I in England and tirelessly working to secure his ransom, demonstrating an unyielding spirit.
translatedText: Bayan da aka sake ta daga ɗaurin kurkuku, ta ci gaba da yin tasiri a siyasance, tana aiki a matsayin mai mulki ga ɗanta Richard I a Ingila kuma ta yi aiki tuƙuru don ta sami fansa, tana nuna ruhu mai jajircewa.

sourceText: Middle Ages
translatedText: Tsakanin Zamani

sourceText: Sundiata Keita
translatedText: Sundiata Keita

sourceText: As a child, I was crippled and an outcast, my right to the throne challenged by my stepmother and her sons. I was forced into exile, facing constant threats and the despair of a lost homeland.
translatedText: Lokacin da nake yaro, na kasance gurgu ne kuma na zama wanda aka yi watsi da ni, haƙƙina na zama karagar mulki da mahaifiyata da ’ya’yanta suka ƙalubalanci. An tilasta ni gudun hijira, ina fuskantar barazana da kuma fidda rai na ƙasar da aka rasa.

sourceText: Yet, the spirit of the lion could not be caged. During my exile, I forged alliances and gathered loyal warriors. I returned with a formidable army, reclaimed my birthright, and united the Mandinka people, transforming them into a formidable empire.
translatedText: Duk da haka, ruhun zaki ya kasa rufewa. Sa’ad da nake gudun hijira, na ƙulla kawance kuma na tara mayaka masu aminci. Na dawo da runduna mai ƙarfi, na kwato matsayina na ɗan fari, na haɗa kan mutanen Mandinka, na mai da su babbar daula.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 13

sourceText: Maria Theresa
translatedText: Mariya Theresa

sourceText: She faced prejudice and challenges as a female ruler in a male-dominated society and endured severe national security and economic difficulties due to intense territorial disputes with Prussia (War of the Austrian Succession, Seven Years' War).
translatedText: Ta fuskanci wariya da kalubale a matsayinta na mace mai mulki a cikin al'ummar da maza suka mamaye kuma ta jimre da matsalolin tsaro na kasa da kuma matsalolin tattalin arziki saboda tsananin rikice-rikice na yankuna tare da Prussia (Yakin Nasarar Austrian, Yakin Shekaru Bakwai).

sourceText: She overcame crises through strong foreign policy and military reforms, and strengthened the empire's centralization by reforming education and judicial systems, improving the lives of her subjects.
translatedText: Ta shawo kan rikice-rikice ta hanyar manufofin kasashen waje masu karfi da gyare-gyare na soja, kuma ta karfafa tsarin daular ta hanyar yin kwaskwarima ga ilimi da tsarin shari'a, inganta rayuwar al'ummarta.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: After her husband's death, the Romans seized the Iceni lands, flogged Boudicca, and violated her daughters. This profound injustice ignited her fury and resolve for vengeance.
translatedText: Bayan mutuwar mijinta, Romawa sun kwace ƙasar Iceni, suka yi wa Boudicca bulala, kuma suka ci zarafin ’ya’yanta mata. Wannan babban rashin adalci ya sa ta huce haushinta da azamar daukar fansa.

sourceText: Fueled by this immense suffering and humiliation, Boudicca rallied various British tribes, launching a fierce rebellion. Though ultimately defeated, her resistance struck terror into the Roman invaders and inspired future generations.
translatedText: Sakamakon wannan gagarumin wahala da wulakanci, Boudicca ya tara kabilu daban-daban na Biritaniya, ya kaddamar da tawaye mai tsanani. Ko da yake an ci nasara a ƙarshe, juriyarta ta afka wa mahara na Romawa ta'addanci kuma ta ƙarfafa tsararraki masu zuwa.

sourceText: 1st Century AD
translatedText: Karni na 1 AD

sourceText: She began as a prostitute and was initially subservient to her husband, Zheng Yi. Her fleet also faced constant threats from the Qing government and Portuguese and British naval forces.
translatedText: Ta fara a matsayin karuwa kuma ta kasance mai biyayya ga mijinta, Zheng Yi. Rundunar ta kuma na fuskantar barazana a kai a kai daga gwamnatin Qing da sojojin ruwan Portugal da na Birtaniya.

sourceText: After Zheng Yi's death, she seized control of his fleet and consolidated her power. Through strict codes and brilliant strategy, she suppressed internal dissent and repelled external threats, expanding her influence.
translatedText: Bayan mutuwar Zheng Yi, ta kwace iko da rundunarsa tare da karfafa ikonta. Ta hanyar tsauraran lambobi da dabaru masu kyau, ta murkushe rashin amincewa na cikin gida tare da tunkude barazanar waje, tana fadada tasirinta.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: He faced challenges in consolidating power after overthrowing the previous ruler, Sonni Ali, and dealt with internal dissent and the constant threat of rebellion across a vast territory.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci kalubale wajen tabbatar da mulki bayan ya hambarar da mulkin da ya gabata, Sonni Ali, ya kuma magance rashin amincewar cikin gida da kuma barazanar tawaye a kodayaushe a fadin kasa mai fadi.

sourceText: Askia the Great implemented a strong centralized administration, standardized weights and measures, established a sophisticated bureaucracy, and promoted Islam as a unifying force. His pilgrimage to Mecca in 1496 also enhanced his prestige and solidified his legitimacy.
translatedText: Askia the Great ta aiwatar da tsarin mulki mai ƙarfi, daidaita ma'auni da ma'auni, kafa tsarin tsarin mulki na zamani, da haɓaka Musulunci a matsayin wani ƙarfi na haɗin kai. Ziyarar da ya yi zuwa Makka a shekara ta 1496 shi ma ya kara masa daraja da kuma tabbatar da halaccinsa.

sourceText: Late 15th - Early 16th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 15th - Farkon Ƙarni na 16

sourceText: Frequent political upheavals and wars created an unstable research environment, forcing him to move between various regions.
translatedText: Rikicin siyasa da yaƙe-yaƙe da yaƙe-yaƙe ya ​​haifar da yanayin bincike mara kyau, wanda ya tilasta masa tafiya tsakanin yankuna daban-daban.

sourceText: He never lost his passion for learning, regardless of the adversity. He overcame challenges through constant observation, meticulous documentation, and a dedicated effort to synthesize knowledge from different cultures.
translatedText: Bai taɓa rasa sha'awar koyo ba, ba tare da la'akari da wahala ba. Ya shawo kan ƙalubale ta hanyar sa ido akai-akai, ƙwaƙƙwaran rubuce-rubuce, da ƙoƙarin haɗa ilimi daga al'adu daban-daban.

sourceText: 10th-11th Century
translatedText: Karni na 10-11

sourceText: William Harvey
translatedText: William Harvey

sourceText: The entrenched dogma of Galen, the skepticism from the established scientific community towards novel ideas, and the arduous task of gathering irrefutable evidence for my theories presented significant challenges.
translatedText: Tushen koyarwar Galen, da shakku daga kafaffen al'ummar kimiyya game da sabbin ra'ayoyi, da aiki mai wahala na tattara shaidun da ba za a iya warwarewa ba game da ra'ayoyina sun gabatar da ƙalubale masu mahimmanci.

sourceText: Through meticulous dissection and experimentation, I presented undeniable proof that blood circulates from the heart, through the body, and back again. My persistent dedication and scientific inquiry eventually earned acceptance within the medical world.
translatedText: Ta hanyar rarrabuwar kawuna da gwaji, na gabatar da hujjar da ba za ta iya musantawa ba cewa jini na yawo daga zuciya, ta jiki, da kuma baya. Nacewa sadaukarwa da binciken kimiyya daga ƙarshe ya sami karɓuwa a cikin duniyar likitanci.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: Joseph Priestley
translatedText: Joseph Priestley

sourceText: Priestley faced significant social and political opposition due to his radical religious and political views. His support for the French Revolution and his dissenting stance against the Church of England led to the destruction of his church and property.
translatedText: Priestley ya fuskanci gagarumin adawa na zamantakewa da siyasa saboda ra'ayinsa na addini da na siyasa. Goyon bayansa ga juyin juya halin Faransa da ra'ayinsa na rashin amincewa da Cocin Ingila ya kai ga lalata cocinsa da dukiyoyinsa.

sourceText: Despite persecution, Priestley persevered in his scientific research. He emigrated to the United States, continuing his work in a new environment, and his scientific contributions were eventually recognized and celebrated.
translatedText: Duk da tsanantawa, Priestley ya dage a bincikensa na kimiyya. Ya yi hijira zuwa Amurka, ya ci gaba da aikinsa a cikin wani sabon yanayi, kuma a ƙarshe an gane gudummawar da ya bayar a kimiyya kuma an yi bikin.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Linnaeus faced significant financial hardship and academic challenges early in his career. His groundbreaking ideas on classifying life were also met with skepticism and resistance from established scientific circles.
translatedText: Linnaeus ya fuskanci babban matsalar kuɗi da ƙalubalen ilimi a farkon aikinsa. Har ila yau, ra'ayoyinsa masu ban sha'awa game da rarraba rayuwa sun gamu da shakku da tsayin daka daga kafafan da'irar kimiyya.

sourceText: Through relentless observation, extensive fieldwork across Europe, and meticulous documentation, Linnaeus refined his system. His persistence and the undeniable utility of his binomial nomenclature eventually led to its widespread adoption and cemented his legacy.
translatedText: Ta hanyar lura da hankali, aikin fage da yawa a duk faɗin Turai, da ƙwararrun takaddun bayanai, Linnaeus ya inganta tsarinsa. Dagewar da ya yi da kuma rashin amfani da sunan sa na binomial daga ƙarshe ya kai ga yaɗuwar ta kuma ya tabbatar da gadonsa.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Lise Meitner
translatedText: Lise Meitner

sourceText: She faced academic limitations due to her gender and persecution from the Nazi regime because she was Jewish, forcing her into exile. She also experienced the pain of not being recognized for her contribution to the discovery of nuclear fission and not receiving the Nobel Prize.
translatedText: Ta fuskanci gazawar ilimi saboda jinsinta da tsanantawa daga gwamnatin Nazi domin ita Bayahudiya ce, ta tilasta mata yin hijira. Ta kuma fuskanci radadin rashin sanin irin gudunmawar da ta bayar wajen gano fasa kwaurin nukiliya da rashin samun lambar yabo ta Nobel.

sourceText: Despite adverse circumstances, she never lost her passion for science. She continued her research even in exile and, through collaboration with fellow scientists, completed the theoretical explanation of nuclear fission. Although she did not receive the Nobel Prize, she left a significant mark on the scientific community.
translatedText: Duk da munanan yanayi, ba ta daina sha'awar kimiyya ba. Ta ci gaba da bincikenta har ma a gudun hijira kuma, ta hanyar haɗin gwiwa tare da masana kimiyya, ta kammala bayanin ka'idar fission na nukiliya. Ko da yake ba ta sami lambar yabo ta Nobel ba, ta bar wata muhimmiyar alama ga al'ummar kimiyya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: Emmy Noether
translatedText: Emmy Noether

sourceText: Persecution under the Nazi regime and the discrimination and exclusion she faced in academia due to her Jewish heritage.
translatedText: Tsanantawa a ƙarƙashin mulkin Nazi da wariya da wariya da ta fuskanta a fannin ilimi saboda gadonta na Yahudawa.

sourceText: After emigrating to the United States, she secured a professorship at Bryn Mawr College, continuing her research and inspiring many young mathematicians.
translatedText: Bayan ta yi hijira zuwa Amurka, ta sami digiri na Farfesa a Kwalejin Bryn Mawr, ta ci gaba da bincike da kuma zaburar da matasa masu ilmin lissafi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Alexander von Humboldt
translatedText: Alexander von Humboldt asalin

sourceText: He experienced the early loss of his mother and occasional friction with his brother Wilhelm. His expeditions were often fraught with danger and physically demanding conditions.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci farkon asarar mahaifiyarsa da kuma rikice-rikice na lokaci-lokaci tare da ɗan'uwansa Wilhelm. Yawancin balaguronsa na cike da haɗari da yanayi masu wuyar gaske.

sourceText: Humboldt's insatiable curiosity and relentless drive for discovery propelled him forward. Through meticulous research, detailed documentation, and collaboration with fellow scientists, he overcame obstacles and solidified his scientific achievements.
translatedText: Humboldt na rashin gamsuwa da son sani da kuma neman ganowa ya sa shi gaba. Ta hanyar bincike mai zurfi, cikakkun bayanai, da haɗin gwiwa tare da masana kimiyya, ya shawo kan cikas kuma ya ƙarfafa nasarorin kimiyya.

sourceText: Late 18th - Mid 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 18th - Tsakiyar Karni na 19

sourceText: Tycho Brahe
translatedText: Tycho Brahe

sourceText: The conflict between his duties as a nobleman in military and political affairs versus his passion for scholarship. The lifelong complex stemming from the loss of his nose in a duel, and the rivalries and envy from fellow scientists.
translatedText: Rikicin da ke tsakanin aikinsa na mai martaba a harkokin soja da siyasa da sha’awar neman gurbin karatu. Rikicin rayuwa wanda ya samo asali daga asarar hancinsa a cikin duel, da kishiyoyi da hassada daga abokan masana kimiyya.

sourceText: He channeled his talents and passion into astronomical research, achieving significant academic accomplishments and gaining public acclaim. He also attempted to overcome his appearance complex by crafting a metal nose and using masks.
translatedText: Ya ba da basirarsa da sha'awarsa a cikin binciken sararin samaniya, yana samun nasarori masu mahimmanci na ilimi da samun yabo ga jama'a. Ya kuma yi yunƙurin shawo kan kamannin sa ta hanyar kera hancin ƙarfe da kuma amfani da abin rufe fuska.

sourceText: 16th Century
translatedText: Karni na 16

sourceText: Muir endured a strict, labor-intensive upbringing in Scotland, suffering frequent injuries and illnesses. A severe factory accident in 1867 nearly cost him his eyesight, a profound physical and emotional trial.
translatedText: Muir ya jimre da tsattsauran ra'ayi, renon aiki a Scotland, yana fama da raunuka da cututtuka akai-akai. Wani mummunan hatsarin masana'anta a cikin 1867 ya kusan kashe masa gani, babban gwaji na jiki da na zuciya.

sourceText: Confined to darkness after his accident, Muir's recovery was deeply intertwined with his rediscovery of the natural world through memory and imagination. This near-blindness paradoxically sharpened his spiritual connection to nature, fueling his lifelong advocacy for its preservation.
translatedText: An kulle shi cikin duhu bayan hatsarin da ya yi, murmurwar Muir ya kasance da alaƙa sosai tare da sake gano yanayin duniyar ta hanyar ƙwaƙwalwa da tunani. Wannan makaho na kusa ya kara kaifafa dangantakarsa ta ruhaniya da yanayi, yana kara ba da shawararsa na tsawon rayuwarsa don kiyaye ta.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Alfred Russel Wallace
translatedText: Alfred Russel Wallace

sourceText: Financial struggles, bouts of serious illness, and the challenge of pursuing scientific inquiry often without immediate recognition or support.
translatedText: Gwagwarmayar kudi, fama da rashin lafiya mai tsanani, da ƙalubalen neman binciken kimiyya sau da yawa ba tare da sanin ko goyan baya nan take ba.

sourceText: His profound passion for nature, resilience in the face of adversity, and unwavering dedication to scientific observation allowed him to persevere and make monumental contributions to our understanding of life.
translatedText: Babban sha'awarsa ga yanayi, juriya a fuskantar wahala, da sadaukar da kai ga lura da kimiyya sun ba shi damar dagewa da ba da gudummawa mai mahimmanci ga fahimtar rayuwa.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Pierre Curie
translatedText: Pierre Kuri

sourceText: He faced financial struggles, lacked adequate laboratory equipment, and suffered from health issues related to his research.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci gwagwarmayar kudi, rashin isassun kayan aikin dakin gwaje-gwaje, kuma ya sha fama da matsalolin lafiya da suka shafi bincikensa.

sourceText: Through persistent effort and collaboration with his wife, Marie Curie, he overcame these challenges and achieved significant scientific breakthroughs.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ci gaba da ƙoƙari da haɗin gwiwa tare da matarsa, Marie Curie, ya shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen kuma ya sami ci gaba na kimiyya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Charles Lyell
translatedText: Charles Lyell ne adam wata

sourceText: Lyell faced significant opposition from the scientific establishment for his radical theory of uniformitarianism, which challenged prevailing views of Earth's history. Gathering sufficient evidence to support his ideas, often in remote and challenging locations, also presented considerable hardship and academic isolation.
translatedText: Lyell ya fuskanci babban adawa daga kafa kimiyya don ka'idarsa mai tsattsauran ra'ayi na uniformitarianism, wanda ya kalubalanci ra'ayoyin tarihin duniya. Tara isassun shaidu don tallafa wa ra'ayoyinsa, sau da yawa a wurare masu nisa da ƙalubale, ya kuma gabatar da wahala mai yawa da keɓewar ilimi.

sourceText: Through meticulous fieldwork and the systematic accumulation of evidence, Lyell steadfastly defended his principles. 'Principles of Geology' became the definitive text for decades, and his concept of gradual change profoundly influenced not only geology but also evolutionary biology, eventually earning him widespread recognition and respect.
translatedText: Ta hanyar aikin fage mai kyau da kuma tarin shaidu na tsari, Lyell ya ci gaba da kare ƙa'idodinsa. 'Ka'idodin Geology' ya zama tabbataccen rubutu tsawon shekaru da yawa, kuma ra'ayinsa na canji a hankali ya yi tasiri sosai ba kawai ilimin ƙasa ba har ma da ilimin halitta, wanda a ƙarshe ya sami karɓuwa da girmamawa sosai.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Al-Farabi faced significant challenges due to the religious and political turmoil of his era, often finding his philosophical ideas misunderstood or criticized. He navigated a world where intellectual pursuits could be fraught with peril.
translatedText: Al-Farabi dai ya fuskanci kalubale masu yawa saboda rikicin addini da na siyasa a zamaninsa, inda a lokuta da dama yakan sami fahimtar ra'ayinsa na falsafa ko suka. Ya zagaya duniya inda neman ilimi zai iya cika da hadari.

sourceText: Through relentless scholarship and prolific writing, he meticulously developed his philosophical system. He engaged with leading intellectuals of his time, disseminating his ideas and securing a lasting legacy of profound wisdom that influenced generations.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ba da guraben karatu da ƙwararrun rubuce-rubuce, ya haɓaka tsarin falsafarsa sosai. Ya shagaltu da manyan masana na zamaninsa, tare da yada ra'ayoyinsa da samun dawwamammen gado na hikima mai zurfi wanda ya yi tasiri ga tsararraki.

sourceText: 10th Century
translatedText: Karni na 10

sourceText: Thomas More
translatedText: Thomas More

sourceText: He faced immense pressure from King Henry VIII to accept his divorce and acknowledge him as the Supreme Head of the Church of England. This defiance of his religious convictions led to his imprisonment in the Tower of London and eventual execution.
translatedText: Ya fuskanci matsananciyar matsi daga Sarki Henry na VIII ya amince da kashe aurensa kuma ya amince da shi a matsayin Babban Shugaban Cocin Ingila. Wannan bijirewa hukuncin da aka yanke masa na addini ya sa aka daure shi a Hasumiyar Landan kuma daga bisani aka yanke masa hukuncin kisa.

sourceText: Despite the threat of torture and death, More remained steadfast in his conscience and beliefs. He chose martyrdom to uphold his principles, leaving a legacy that continues to inspire.
translatedText: Duk da barazanar azabtarwa da kisa, More ya kasance da tsayin daka cikin lamirinsa da imaninsa. Ya zabi shahada don kiyaye ka'idojinsa, ya bar gadon da ke ci gaba da zaburarwa.

sourceText: Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance

sourceText: Simone Weil
translatedText: Simone Weil
